has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: mixpanel__daily_events
metadata.comment:
Table of each **event type's** daily history of activity, as reflected in user retention and event metrics.
Default materialization is incremental.

date_day.name: date_day
date_day.comment: Calendar day of the logged events (using the timezone set in your Mixpanel project).

event_type.name: event_type
event_type.comment: Name of the type of event tracked by Mixpanel.

number_of_events.name: number_of_events
number_of_events.comment: The number of events logged for this event type on a given day.

number_of_users.name: number_of_users
number_of_users.comment: The number of unique users who triggered this kind of a event on a given day.

number_of_new_users.name: number_of_new_users
number_of_new_users.comment: The number of unique users who are performing this kind of event for the very first time (not affected by `date_range_start`).

number_of_repeat_users.name: number_of_repeat_users
number_of_repeat_users.comment: The number of unique users who also performed this event in the previous 28 days.

number_of_return_users.name: number_of_return_users
number_of_return_users.comment: The number of unique users who have performed this event before, but not recently (not in the previous 28 days).

trailing_users_28d.name: trailing_users_28d
trailing_users_28d.comment: The number of unique users who have completed this kind of event in the past 28 days.

trailing_users_7d.name: trailing_users_7d
trailing_users_7d.comment: The number of unique users who have completed this kind of event in the past 7 days.

unique_key.name: unique_key
unique_key.comment: Unique key used for the incremental model configuration. Concats `event_type`, `date_day` and `source_relation`

dbt_run_date.name: dbt_run_date
dbt_run_date.comment: The date of the dbt run when the record was added.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: mixpanel__event
metadata.comment:
Table of de-duplicated Mixpanel events. Calls on the `pivot_event_properties_json` macro to  turn any properties specified by the `event_properties` variable into columns. 
Default materialization is incremental.

unique_event_id.name: unique_event_id
unique_event_id.comment: Unique ID of the event. Events are de-duped according to Mixpanel's [requirements](https://developer.mixpanel.com/reference/http#event-deduplication). This is hashed on `insert_id`, `people_id`, `date_day`, `source_relation`, and `event_type`

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: UTC timestamp of when the row was last successfully synced.

app_session_length.name: app_session_length
app_session_length.comment: The length of the user's mobile app session. This is captured as a property of  the default 'App Session' mobile event tracked by mixpanel (triggers after > 10 seconds  in the app).

has_bluetooth_enabled.name: has_bluetooth_enabled
has_bluetooth_enabled.comment: Boolean that is true if Bluetooth is enabled, false if not.

wireless_carrier.name: wireless_carrier
wireless_carrier.comment: Wireless carrier of the device owner.

city.name: city
city.comment: The city of the event sender, as parsed from the IP property or the Latitude and Longitude properties.

current_url.name: current_url
current_url.comment: The full URL of the webpage on which the event was triggered.

device_name.name: device_name
device_name.comment: The name of the event sender's device, if they're on mobile web.

device_id.name: device_id
device_id.comment: A unique string that identifies a user before an authentication or identification flow.  By default, Mixpanel creates a device_id for every unique browser/device.

people_id.name: people_id
people_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the event's associated user's `people.distinct_id`.

people_id_before_identified.name: people_id_before_identified
people_id_before_identified.comment: When an event comes in with a distinct_id that has been remapped to something  else, this property contains the original value to aid debugging and error solution.

google_play_service_status.name: google_play_service_status
google_play_service_status.comment: Verifies that Google Play services is installed and enabled on this device,  and that the version installed on this device is no older than the one required by this client.

has_near_field_communication.name: has_near_field_communication
has_near_field_communication.comment: Boolean that is true if Near Field Communication is being used, false if not.

has_telephone.name: has_telephone
has_telephone.comment: Boolean that is true if this device has telephone functionality, false if not.

initial_referrer.name: initial_referrer
initial_referrer.comment: The referring URL at first arrival.

initial_referring_domain.name: initial_referring_domain
initial_referring_domain.comment: The referring domain at first arrival.

insert_id.name: insert_id
insert_id.comment: Random 16 character string of alphanumeric characters that is unique to an event.  Used to de-duplicate data.

mixpanel_library_version.name: mixpanel_library_version
mixpanel_library_version.comment: Mixpanel library used to send this data.

country_code.name: country_code
country_code.comment: The country code of the event sender, as parsed from the IP property or the Latitude and Longitude  properties.

referrer_keywords.name: referrer_keywords
referrer_keywords.comment: Search keywords detected on the referrer from a search engine to your domain.  This property is only collected when search keywords are included in a URL.

mixpanel_library.name: mixpanel_library
mixpanel_library.comment: Mixpanel Library that sent the event.

mp_processing_time_ms.name: mp_processing_time_ms
mp_processing_time_ms.comment: Time in milliseconds in Unix Processing Time in which the event was ingested by Mixpanel.

event_type_original_casing.name: event_type_original_casing
event_type_original_casing.comment: Name of the type of event that was logged, in its orginal capitalization.

os.name: os
os.comment: Operating system of the event sender.

os_version.name: os_version
os_version.comment: Current version of iOS or Android on the device.

event_properties.name: event_properties
event_properties.comment: JSON capturing properties specific to this type of event. Properties listed in the `event_properties` variable will be turned into columns in the `mixpanel__event` model.

network_type.name: network_type
network_type.comment: The current cellular network communication standard type (3G, 4G, LTE, etc.) of the device  that produced the event.

referrer.name: referrer
referrer.comment: Referring URL, including your own domain.

referring_domain.name: referring_domain
referring_domain.comment: Referring domain, including your own domain.

region.name: region
region.comment: The region (state or province) of the event sender, as parsed from the IP property or the Latitude and  Longitude properties.

screen_pixel_density.name: screen_pixel_density
screen_pixel_density.comment: Pixel density (dots per inch) of the device screen.

screen_height.name: screen_height
screen_height.comment: The height of the device screen in pixels (web or android) or points (ios).

screen_width.name: screen_width
screen_width.comment: Width of the device screen in pixels (web or android) or points (ios).

search_engine.name: search_engine
search_engine.comment: Search engine a customer used when they arrived at your domain.

occurred_at.name: occurred_at
occurred_at.comment: Timestamp of when the event was recorded. Set in the timezone specified by the mixpanel project.

has_wifi_connected.name: has_wifi_connected
has_wifi_connected.comment: Boolean that is true if the event user’s device has an active, available Wifi connection,  and false if not.

date_day.name: date_day
date_day.comment: Calendar day of when the event occurred.

event_type.name: event_type
event_type.comment: Name of the type of event that was logged, in lowercase.

nth_event_record.name: nth_event_record
nth_event_record.comment: Window-function generated field used to dedupe events. Will always = 1.

dbt_run_date.name: dbt_run_date
dbt_run_date.comment: The date of the dbt run when the record was added.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: mixpanel__monthly_events
metadata.comment:
Table of each **event type's** monthly history of activity, as reflected in user retention and event metrics.
Default materialization is incremental.

date_month.name: date_month
date_month.comment: Month-year of the logged events (using the timezone set in your Mixpanel project).

event_type.name: event_type
event_type.comment: Name of the type of event tracked by Mixpanel.

total_monthly_active_users.name: total_monthly_active_users
total_monthly_active_users.comment: The total number of users who performed **any kind of event** this month (regardless of event type).

number_of_users.name: number_of_users
number_of_users.comment: The number of unique users who triggered this kind of a event in a month.

number_of_new_users.name: number_of_new_users
number_of_new_users.comment: The number of unique users who performed this kind of event for the first time this month (not affected by `date_range_start`).

number_of_repeat_users.name: number_of_repeat_users
number_of_repeat_users.comment: The number of unique users who also performed this event in the previous calendar month.

number_of_return_users.name: number_of_return_users
number_of_return_users.comment: The number of unique users who have performed this event before, but not recently (in the last calendar month).

number_of_events.name: number_of_events
number_of_events.comment: The number of events logged for this event type in a given month.

number_of_churn_users.name: number_of_churn_users
number_of_churn_users.comment: The number of unique users who performed this kind of event last month, but not this month.

unique_key.name: unique_key
unique_key.comment: Unique key used for the incremental model configuration. Concats `event_type`, `date_month` and `source_relation`

dbt_run_date.name: dbt_run_date
dbt_run_date.comment: The date of the dbt run when the record was added.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: mixpanel__sessions
metadata.comment:
Table aggregating events into unique user sessions, according to the `sessionization_inactivity` timeout length. Contains any additional columns specified by the `session_passthrough_columns` variable. Note that sessions are  specific to the device, as opposed to the user.
Default materialization is incremental.

session_id.name: session_id
session_id.comment: Unique ID of the user session. Hashed on `user_id`, `session_number`, `date_day`, and `source_relation`

people_id.name: people_id
people_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the event's associated user's `people.distinct_id`.

session_started_at.name: session_started_at
session_started_at.comment: Timestamp of when the session began (in the timezone of the Mixpanel project).

session_started_on_day.name: session_started_on_day
session_started_on_day.comment: Calendar date of when the session began.

device_id.name: device_id
device_id.comment: A unique string that identifies a user before an authentication or identification flow.  By default, Mixpanel creates a device_id for every unique browser/device.

total_number_of_events.name: total_number_of_events
total_number_of_events.comment: The total number of events triggered during this user session.

event_frequencies.name: event_frequencies
event_frequencies.comment: JSON of the frequency of each `event_type` during this user session.

dbt_run_date.name: dbt_run_date
dbt_run_date.comment: The date of the dbt run when the record was added.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_mixpanel__user_event_date_spine
metadata.comment: Incrementally materialized table relating each user-event_type combination to a calendar day (starting at the first day they performed the type of event). This is necessary for certain window functions in  `mixpanel__daily_events`.

people_id.name: people_id
people_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the event's associated user's `people.distinct_id`.

event_type.name: event_type
event_type.comment: Name of the type of event that was logged.

is_first_event_day.name: is_first_event_day
is_first_event_day.comment: Boolean value converted to integers. Equals 1 if the user performed this type of event for the first time on this  day.

unique_key.name: unique_key
unique_key.comment: Unique key used for the incremental model configuration. Hashed on `people_id`, `event_type`, `source_relation`, and `date_day`.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_mixpanel__user_first_event
metadata.comment: Table in which each row represents the first day a user performed a type of event.

people_id.name: people_id
people_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the event's associated user's `people.distinct_id`.

event_type.name: event_type
event_type.comment: Name of the type of event that was logged.

first_event_day.name: first_event_day
first_event_day.comment: Calendar date of the first time

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table
